title: kalabotis pallens
intro: Kalabotis pallens, also known as the pale Kalabotis or pale daisy, is a flowering plant species native to the mountainous regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and flowers, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. Kalabotis pallens is said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, making it beneficial for treating various health problems, such as colds, coughs, and skin irritations. Herbal preparations of Kalabotis pallens include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to relieve symptoms and promote overall well-being.
